question_text: Which of the following is an example of a contact force?

options.label: A
options.text: Gravitational force between the Earth and the Moon

options.label: B
options.text: The normal force from a table pushing up on a book

options.label: C
options.text: The magnetic force between two magnets

options.label: D
options.text: Electrostatic force between two charged balloons

explanation: Contact forces happen when two objects touch. The normal force from a table pushing up on a book is a contact force because the table physically touches the book. Gravitational, magnetic, and electrostatic forces are non-contact forces that act at a distance.
